source_display: AI as Brain Crutch for ADHD
evidence: Vrijen shared his personal interest in building AI tools as 'brain crutches' for ADHD—filling gaps between therapy, medication, and coaching
Chris revealed he's been building his edtech product for almost a year, leveraging AI despite being a former special education teacher rather than a developer
Vrijen shared advice from his edtech experience: avoid the 'death by a thousand cuts' of endless unpaid pilots. Instead, seek paid pilots with scoped changes
Strong reaction when V warned about investors. He doesn't want VC money... Bootstrapper mindset; prioritizes autonomy; prefers organic growth through customers

Chris is Heather's colleague at their coaching practice, and this connection established the context for their meeting. Vrijen currently receiving executive function coaching from Chris's colleague Heather
He defined product-market fit using the Supreme Court's obscenity standard: 'you know it when you see it'—when you have it, you don't think about it; when you don't, you obsess over it constantly

source_display: Building in Public for Emotive Spaces
evidence: Vrijen recommended building in public given the emotive nature of executive function coaching
The core concept is a 'marketplace of all possible futures' - allowing users to 'jump into' career simulations mapped to real jobs and companies

source_display: Jobs as Getting Paid to Learn
evidence: He explained that jobs are essentially 'getting paid to learn,' but gatekeeping makes experience inaccessible
Max used the metaphor of a 'neopet' - anytime you train or deploy your AI requires 'energy,' which is where they monetize through usage-based pricing
The consensus is to aim for a sale mid-year but spend the next few months generating revenue first, which Vrijen believes will massively improve their valuation

source_display: Assessment Tools Don't Ground Business Value
evidence: HackerRank... really robust assessment tool I think is a piece of shit service because it just doesn't baseline or ground the value on anything

source_display: Visible Product Connection for AI-Era Survival
evidence: Whatever you do on your work has to be some sort of visible link between you and the service or product that's being sold. Because otherwise, like what?

source_display: PM Role Obsolescence in AI Era
evidence: These roles are 'done' in the current AI-enabled landscape, with many PM positions facing similar elimination unless they manage P&L and have product experience
Vrijen described a self-fulfilling prophecy where increased hassle leads to corner-cutting, which then worsens outcomes and creates more hassle
He introduced context engineering as the foundational practice: not prompting the AI directly, but cultivating the context it uses to generate desired outputs. He outlined three strategic levels

source_display: AI Stuck Users Have Mental Model Problem
evidence: Vrijen argued the core issue isn't a technology or tactics problem but a mental model problem—users collect techniques without understanding how they integrate into effective systems
Threshold rubrics: Ask the AI to create an evaluation rubric, fill it out, assign scores, and iterate output until achieving the highest level
You can unhobble your AI a lot when you give it access to your files and data... when you give the AI its own home
Logan demonstrated deep analysis of 18 years of LinkedIn data using Claude Code, having exported 48 CSV files containing 5,000+ messages, connections, search history

source_display: Job Security is Dead, Build Career Security
evidence: Job security is dead and you need to be building career security for yourself... it has a lot to do with being findable and being visible

source_display: Plan Files as Future of Coding
evidence: He positioned this as the future of coding: planning systems through narrative structure rather than pure code alone
Come up with like two to three things that would really delight me... what's LinkedIn sitting on that they should be showing us
Ben describes Vrijen as 'really amazing Zo user, kind of one of our power users'
Vrijen and Logan comfortable presenting together as co-founders of Careerspan in AI productivity workshop

source_display: Posting Frequency to Inbound Connection Correlation
evidence: Documented 1,800% increase in inbound connections when increasing posting frequency, strong evidence for visibility strategies
The team has been reduced to three people, with two founders unpaid. The consensus is to aim for a sale mid-year but spend the next few months generating revenue first
